rulings.Case Title: JULES RADCLIFFE et al VS. PHUONG PHAM et al
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 1, 2014, line 1. DEFENDANT PHUONG PHAM's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT Sustained in part and overruled in part. Demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to attach the contracts, to allege relevant provisions verbatim, or to allege the legal effect of the relevant provisions. Demurrer to the 4th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend. Reservation of rights clause in the Settlement Agreement and statements made in the Jellison's letter are not an enforceable contractual provision against defendants. Demurrer to the ninth cause of action is overruled. Demurrer is not a proper vehicle to address factual issues of defendant's good/bad faith. (Demurrer 9:11-15). = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: THIRD AND MISSION ASOCIATES, LLC VS. FANYA YOUNG et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn For Dismissal; Memo Of P & A And Dec.
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 1, 2014, line 3. DEFENDANT FANYA YOUNG's Motion For Dismissal DENIED. Moving party failed to sustain burden and failed to show that her tenancy is governed by federal notice requirements. Defendant also failed to provide any evidence or argument suggesting that Plaintiff failed to comply with the notice requirements. Additionally this Unlawful Detainer was based on just cause of non-payment of rent and judgment entered was pursuant to a breach of stipulation. Finally, arguments raised in the Supplement to Motion fro Dismissal filed on July 31, 2014 do not support the dismissal/setting aside judgment. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: ROSALIE WARD VS. FRANK X. DONNER et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant; Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, August 1, 2014, line 5. PLAINTIFF ROSALIE WARD's Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, To Establish Admissions From Defendant, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant GRANTED, subject to opposition. Defendant shall provide responses by personal delivery or overnight mail to Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories and Requests for Production within 5 days of notice of entry of order. Admissions are deemed admitted. Sanctions granted in the amount of $850. = (501/CFH)
